name: Muslim Community Network
mission: We are dedicated to using civic education and leadership development to shape the public narrative about what it means to be Muslim in America.

geographicCoverage: New York City (New York, United States)
Brooklyn (Kings County, New York, United States)

theoryOfChange: Equipping community leaders and frontline staff with cultural competency and safety tools leads to safer, stronger, and more civically engaged communities.
scoreSummary: MCN shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mcnny.org/donate (Source: https://mcnny.org/donate)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 6.9% of revenue ($82,006 on $1,193,572)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

score_details.score_summary: MCN shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mcnny.org/donate (Source: https://mcnny.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: MCN reported total revenue of $1,193,572 and operates programs in youth leadership and civic engagement.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and follows specific zakat policies and asnaf categories.
MCN provides various donation options and giving methods for supporters.
MCN holds a 98.0/100 score, with a 72.9% program expense ratio, 8.2 months of working capital, and fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: MCN demonstrates excellent financial health and transparency, though its overall score is moderated by its focus on long-term capacity building rather than immediate, high-urgency humanitarian intervention.
areas_for_improvement: Limited direct impact data regarding cost per beneficiary
Moderate alignment for donors seeking high-urgency humanitarian relief versus long-term civic capacity building

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization offers a medium fit for donors, balancing religious cultural urgency with long-term civic goals [4]. It provides clear pathways for Zakat-eligible giving, supporting specific asnaf categories through its community education initiatives [2][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: MCN focuses on capacity building through its Youth Leadership Development and Civic Engagement programs [1]. While it maintains a strong program expense ratio of 72.9% and high financial health ratings, specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not currently available to fully quantify its direct effectiveness [4].

baseline_narrative.headline: MCN empowers the American Muslim community through civic education and leadership development to reshape the public narrative.
strengths: High financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 98.0/100
Exceptional fundraising efficiency at $0.03 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 8.2 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: MCN is a New York-based organization dedicated to shaping the public narrative of Muslims in America through youth leadership, community education, and civic engagement [1][4]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through its community-focused programs [2].

all_citations.quote: dedicated to using civic education and leadership development

all_citations.claim: overall score of 98.0/100 from Charity Navigator

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: MCN demonstrates excellent financial health and transparency, though its overall score is moderated by its focus on long-term capacity building rather than immediate, high-urgency humanitarian intervention.

areas_for_improvement.context: While MCN tracks outcomes, there is <cite id="4">limited public data regarding cost-per-beneficiary</cite>, making it difficult to assess comparative effectiveness.
For an organization of this size, the <cite id="1">CEO compensation of $82,006</cite> represents a significant portion of revenue, which may warrant closer board scrutiny.

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB Wise Giving Alliance has not yet completed a full evaluation against all 20 standards.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization is actively tracking three outcome metrics via Candid to improve its evidence base.
risk_factors: <cite id="4">No beneficiary data available</cite> for cost-per-beneficiary scoring
<cite id="1">CEO pay above 5% of revenue</cite> for an organization under $5M
case_against.summary: The primary limitation is the <cite id="4">absence of rigorous third-party impact evaluations</cite> or RCT-level evidence for its capacity-building programs.

confidence.data_freshness: Data current as of 2023 Tax Year and 2024 Ratings
confidence.notes: Strong financial data; impact data is self-reported.

alignment.explanation: MCN is a strong fit for donors prioritizing <cite id="5">Zakat-eligible civic engagement</cite> and local New York City community development.
alignment.improvement: MCN could <cite id="5">clarify its specific zakat allocation policy</cite> to better inform donors on how funds are distributed across eligible categories.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust MCN due to its <cite id="4">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and its <cite id="4">83% independent board</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, MCN could <cite id="4">publish more detailed beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-effectiveness analysis.

impact.explanation: MCN operates efficiently with an <cite id="1">72.9% program expense ratio</cite> and maintains <cite id="4">resilient working capital</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="4">tracking outcomes over at least 3 years</cite> and documenting a clear theory of change.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Outcome tracking via Candid

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Direct service delivery and community training

rich_narrative.headline: MCN empowers the American Muslim community through civic education and leadership development to reshape the public narrative.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value American Muslim civic empowerment and want to support Zakat-eligible community infrastructure in New York City.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through local community development
Believe in reshaping the public narrative about Islam in America
Value high financial transparency and low fundraising overhead
Prioritize youth leadership and civic education
giving_considerations: Focus is regional (NYC) rather than global humanitarian relief
Impact is measured through long-term capacity building rather than immediate crisis aid
Organization is established with nearly two decades of local track record
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-urgency international disaster relief or those requiring rigorous academic RCT evidence.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that MCN <cite id="4">tracks 3 outcome metrics</cite> but lacks external evaluations or long-term longitudinal studies.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MCN creates impact by training youth and community members in civic literacy, assuming that increased engagement will lead to a more equitable public narrative.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that civic training actually translates into increased community representation and narrative shifts.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As a mid-sized organization in a high-cost city, MCN has significant capacity to expand its youth and immigration programs with additional funding.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="2">specific focus on the NYC Muslim narrative</cite> and high financial accountability scores.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Broader focus on health and arts in Chicago
similar_organizations.name: Inner City Muslim Action Network

strengths.detail: MCN has earned <cite id="4">a near-perfect score of 98.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, indicating robust internal controls and transparency.
The organization demonstrates high efficiency, spending only <cite id="4">$0.05 to raise every $1</cite> in contribution revenue.
MCN maintains <cite id="4">8.2 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring significant operational stability and resilience against funding fluctuations.

strengths_deep_dive: MCN's financial health is a cornerstone of its operations, evidenced by a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 72.9%</cite>, which ensures the vast majority of funds are directed toward its core mission of civic empowerment. This efficiency is paired with a <cite id="4">perfect 100/100 score in Culture & Accountability</cite> from third-party evaluators. The organization's ability to maintain <cite id="1">net assets of $506,196</cite> while growing its reach in New York City highlights a sustainable growth model that balances immediate program delivery with long-term institutional health.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Network (MCN) is a New York-based organization dedicated to <cite id="1">shaping the public narrative of Muslims in America</cite> through youth leadership, community education, and civic engagement. Operating primarily within the five boroughs of New York City, MCN focuses on <cite id="2">civic education and leadership development</cite> to empower the American Muslim community. In the 2023 tax year, the organization reported <cite id="1">total revenue of $768,698</cite>, demonstrating a steady growth trajectory with a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 10.9%</cite>. MCN maintains a strong financial standing, characterized by <cite id="4">an overall score of 98.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, which reflects high standards of accountability and transparency. The organization's program model is centered on capacity building, including the <cite id="2">MCN Impact Academy and the Immigration Project</cite>. Furthermore, MCN provides a platform for religious obligation through its <cite id="5">Zakat-eligible programs</cite>, which support various categories of need within the local community, including food drives and emergency assistance.
